name: Thackeray's Lobster (Irish)
recipeInstructions:
(Amounts are approximate: the above measurements should be increased or decreased depending on the size of the lobster. You want about a cup of sauce per two people.)
1. Clean and shell the lobster as indicated.
2. Mix the mustard, vinegar, catsup, and cayenne to taste (you may substitute half-and-half vinegar and white wine).
3. Melt the butter in a large saucepan over medium heat.
4. Saute the lobster briefly in the melted butter, ensuring it does not color.
5. Add the mustard/vinegar/cayenne mixture and mix well.
6. Cover and allow to stew for 15-20 minutes.
7. Serve with boiled new potatoes, baked potatoes, or plain buttered rice.
Unfortunately, three-foot-long lobsters are no longer available in Ireland (though they can still be found on the west coast). Thackeray's friends reportedly drank heavily after enjoying the dish, though the meal itself was sufficient for an emperor.
